name: Don't Buy This Jacket
context: Patagonia placed a full-page ad in the NYT on Black Friday telling consumers NOT to buy their jacket.
insight: The most sustainable product is the one you don't buy.
solution: Full-page NYT ad with 'Don't Buy This Jacket' headline. Launched Common Threads Initiative for repair, reuse, recycle.
results: Sales increased 30%. Brand trust hit record highs. Pioneered anti-consumerism marketing.

name: Real Beauty Campaign
award: Cannes Lions Grand Prix, Effie Grand Prix
context: Only 2% of women worldwide considered themselves beautiful.
insight: Beauty advertising was making women feel worse. A brand celebrating real beauty could transform the category.
solution: Multi-year campaign featuring real women. 'Real Beauty Sketches' became most-watched ad of all time.
results: Sales grew from $2.5B to $4B. Changed beauty industry advertising standards.

name: Dream Crazy ft. Kaepernick
award: Cannes Lions Grand Prix, Emmy Award
context: Colin Kaepernick was blacklisted from the NFL for protesting racial injustice.
insight: Believe in something, even if it means sacrificing everything.
solution: Two-minute brand film narrated by Kaepernick. Outdoor campaign with just his face and the Nike swoosh.
results: $6B brand value increase. 31% online sales spike. Cultural watershed moment.

name: Fearless Girl
award: Cannes Lions Grand Prix x4, D&AD Black Pencil
context: Only 5% of Fortune 500 CEOs were women.
insight: One small statue could challenge the most powerful symbol of capitalism.
solution: Bronze statue of a girl facing Wall Street's Charging Bull, placed on International Women's Day.
results: 1,000+ companies changed board composition. $7.4B in earned media.

name: The E.V.A. Initiative
context: Crash test dummies modeled on male bodies. Women 47% more likely to be seriously injured.
insight: Car safety had a gender bias problem nobody was talking about.
solution: Volvo opened 40 years of safety research to all manufacturers. E.V.A. (Equal Vehicles for All).
results: Changed industry safety standards. Massive global media coverage.

name: Buy Back Friday
context: Black Friday drives excessive consumption.
insight: Instead of discounting new products, reward customers for bringing back old ones.
solution: Customers sold back used IKEA furniture for store credit. Resold in second-hand section.
results: 100,000+ pieces of furniture given second life. Significant media coverage.

name: Parley for the Oceans
context: 8 million tons of plastic enter oceans annually.
insight: Threat = raw material. Ocean plastic is a resource for making better products.
solution: Ultraboost shoes made from recycled ocean plastic. Scaled to millions of pairs.
results: 30M+ Parley shoes sold. Thousands of tons of plastic diverted from oceans.

name: Trash Isles
award: Cannes Lions Grand Prix (PR), D&AD Gold
context: The Great Pacific Garbage Patch is the size of France.
insight: If pollution is a landmass, it deserves the rights of a country.
solution: Applied to UN to recognize garbage patch as country 'Trash Isles'. Al Gore became first citizen.
results: UN attention. 200+ countries saw petition. Changed conversation about ocean plastic.

name: #LikeAGirl
award: Cannes Lions Grand Prix, D&AD Black Pencil
context: 'Like a girl' was universally used as an insult.
insight: When did 'like a girl' become an insult instead of a statement of strength?
solution: Documentary-style film showing how adults vs. young girls interpret 'like a girl'.
results: 85M+ YouTube views. 76% of women no longer saw phrase as negative. Sales grew double digits.

name: True Name
context: Transgender people forced to use deadname on credit cards.
insight: Every transaction is a potential moment of misgendering and humiliation.
solution: Cardholders display chosen True Name without legal name change required.
results: Adopted by major banks. 68% increase in brand favorability among LGBTQ+.

name: The Moldy Whopper
context: Fast food associated with artificial preservatives.
insight: Showing a rotting burger proves it's made with real ingredients.
solution: Time-lapse of Whopper decomposing over 34 days. 'The beauty of no artificial preservatives.'
results: 8.5B impressions. Biggest brand sentiment swing for BK.

name: Xbox Adaptive Controller
award: Cannes Lions Grand Prix, D&AD Impact
context: 400M gamers worldwide have disabilities. Standard controllers unusable for many.
insight: When everybody plays, we all win.
solution: Fully customizable controller for gamers with limited mobility. Super Bowl ad with real gamers.
results: Sold out immediately. Changed accessibility standards across gaming.

name: Good Vibes
context: Deafblind people are among the most isolated in society.
insight: A smartphone can translate between the seeing-hearing world and deafblind world.
solution: App converting text to Morse-like vibrations. Deafblind users communicate via haptic feedback.
results: Changed lives of deafblind community. Open-sourced for other platforms.

name: Shutter Ads
context: COVID forced bars to close. Small owners faced financial ruin.
insight: Every closed shutter is empty advertising space.
solution: Heineken rented shuttered storefronts as ads, paying bar owners their monthly rent.
results: Thousands of bars supported. Brand love scores increased.

name: Post-Gangsta Oat Milk
context: Plant-based milk seen as niche and boring.
insight: Make sustainability the punk rock choice.
solution: Provocative branding, packaging as manifesto, CEO singing badly in Super Bowl ad.
results: Revenue grew from $200M to $700M. IPO at $10B. Changed food brand communication.

name: Project Sunroof
context: Homeowners didn't know if solar panels would work on their roof.
insight: The biggest barrier to solar isn't cost — it's uncertainty.
solution: Google Maps data to calculate solar potential of every rooftop. Free tool with savings estimates.
results: Millions of rooftops analyzed. Significant increase in solar installations.

name: Move to Zero
context: Sportswear has massive carbon footprint.
insight: Zero is the ultimate finish line. Making sustainability a race reframes the conversation.
solution: Platform covering zero carbon/waste. Space Hippie shoes from factory scraps. Refurbished program.
results: 78% renewable energy. 100M+ pounds waste diverted. Space Hippie became cultural moment.

name: Green Hydrogen
context: Heavy industry accounts for 30% of global CO2.
insight: Energy transition isn't just about electricity — it's about replacing fossil fuels where batteries can't go.
solution: Industrial-scale electrolyzers producing green hydrogen. Partnered with steel and chemical industries.
results: Gigawatt-scale projects. Steel decarbonization partnerships. New industrial value chains.

name: Greener Stores
context: 35,000+ stores meant enormous environmental footprint.
insight: A framework that transforms 35,000 stores changes the industry.
solution: 25-standard Greener Store framework. Partnered with WWF for verification.
results: 10,000+ stores certified. 30% energy reduction. Framework shared as open-source.

name: The Ocean Cleanup
context: Ocean plastic cleanup considered impossible at scale.
insight: Let the ocean do the work. Passive systems using currents are exponentially more efficient.
solution: Floating barriers using ocean currents. Interceptor systems in rivers.
results: 500,000+ kg plastic removed. Revenue from recycled products. $100M+ raised.

name: The Amazon Greenventory
context: Natura used AI and drone technology to map and inventory trees in the Amazon rainforest. This innovative approach dramatically accelerated forest surveying from 25 years to just 6 months.
insight: AI and drones can revolutionize conservation efforts by making complex environmental data collection faster and more accessible.
solution: Mapped 400 km² of Amazon using AI-powered drones to create a tree inventory of 30,000 trees and 44 species. This created the largest tree inventory in Amazon history.
results: Completed in 6 months what would traditionally take 25 years, cataloguing 30,000 trees across 400 sq km. Brazil's first Grand Prix in this Lion category.

name: Cars to Work
award: Cannes Lions Grand Prix SDG & Creative Commerce
context: In France, only 1 in 5 unemployed individuals in mobility deserts can accept jobs due to lack of transportation. Renault designed a solution to remove this barrier.
insight: Transportation access is a critical barrier to employment equity in underserved regions. Smart financing models can align business incentives with social outcomes.
solution: Offered long-term car leases through 50 dealerships and 300+ garages. Job seekers only pay after passing probation, choosing from Dacia range or used cars through Renew.
results: Won two Grand Prix awards at Cannes Lions 2024. Partnered with France Travail (government employment agency) reaching nationwide audience.

name: Where to Settle
award: Cannes Lions Grand Prix SDG & Titanium Lion
context: Over 6 million Ukrainian refugees fled to neighboring countries. Many arrived with no information about where to settle, leading to overcrowding in major cities.
insight: Data can be leveraged ethically to solve humanitarian challenges. Anonymized spending patterns reveal livable communities without compromising privacy.
solution: Created web/app platform using anonymized Mastercard spending data to help refugees find ideal locations based on cost of living, job opportunities, and housing availability.
results: 300,000+ refugees used the platform. Zero personal data retained. Won SDG Grand Prix and Titanium Lion at Cannes 2023.

name: Microsoft Cloud for Sustainability
context: Organizations struggle to measure and reduce emissions across complex value chains. Microsoft invested $10 billion in renewable energy capacity to address climate challenges.
insight: AI can automate sustainability reporting and enable data-driven decarbonization at scale.
solution: Developed cloud platform combining data, AI, and IoT to track emissions, set reduction targets, and automate sustainability reporting. Emirates NBD and Allegiant Stadium adopted solutions.
results: Enabled organizations to move from pledges to measurable progress. Diverted 18,537 tonnes of waste in 2023. Trained 14 million people in digital/AI skills for sustainability.

name: Daisy & Taz Recycling Robots
context: Electronics recycling recovers only 5% of rare earth materials. Apple designed robots to increase material recovery and reduce e-waste in landfills.
insight: Automation can increase material recovery rates dramatically, making circular economy economically viable.
solution: Developed Daisy robot (disassembles 36 iPhone models) and Taz machine (recovers rare earth elements from magnets). Opening 9,000-sq-ft Material Recovery Lab in Austin.
results: Reduced aluminum emissions 76% since 2015. 24% of shipped materials from recycled/renewable sources. 60% total GHG reduction vs 2015.

name: Circular Agenda & Buy Back
context: Furniture consumption drives deforestation and waste. IKEA redesigned its business model to extend product lifecycles and enable circularity.
insight: Circular services like buy-back, repair, and resale can increase customer loyalty while reducing environmental impact.
solution: Launched repair services, buy-back programs, second-hand sales, and As-is stores. 60%+ products use renewable materials. 10%+ contain recycled content.
results: 50% GHG reduction target by 2030. Food waste converted to renewable gas and fertilizer. Sweden testing pop-up stores with entirely second-hand products.

name: Fuel-Efficient Routing AI
context: Navigation apps optimize for speed but not fuel efficiency. Google used AI to provide routes that reduce fuel consumption and emissions.
insight: AI can identify hidden inefficiencies in everyday systems, enabling millions to reduce emissions without behavior change.
solution: Deployed AI-powered fuel-efficient routing analyzing traffic, terrain, and engine type. Green Light tool optimizes traffic light timing to reduce stop-go traffic.
results: 2.9 million metric tons GHG emissions avoided since late 2021. Five products enabled 26 million metric tons avoided emissions in 2024.

name: Durham College Geothermal & Smart Campus
context: Buildings account for 40% of global energy use. Siemens deployed smart systems combining renewables, AI, and sensors for deep decarbonization.
insight: Integrated systems combining digital sensors, AI, and renewables can reduce building emissions by 60%+ while improving user experience.
solution: Durham College: Geothermal ground source heat pump system. University of Birmingham: Digital sensors, AI analytics, decentralized energy, renewables. Customers avoided 144 million tons GHG.
results: Durham Building projected 64% GHG reduction. Siemens enabled customers to avoid 144M tons CO2 in 2024. Target: 1,000 megatons by 2030.

name: Energy Access & Decarbonization Programs
context: Energy access and clean energy transition are major barriers in emerging markets. Schneider Electric transformed its business to enable both.
insight: Aligning corporate transformation with social impact creates shared value while achieving net-zero goals.
solution: Partnered with top 1,000 suppliers on carbon reduction. Brought green energy to 50M people. Trained 1M in clean electrification. Invested $1B in startups via Ventures.
results: Ranked #1 globally for sustainability (Corporate Knights 2021). Customers reduced 100M tons CO2 annually. SBTi-validated net-zero targets.
source: Schneider Electric Sustainability Reports & IMD Case Study

name: Sustainable Living Plan & Purpose Brands
context: Consumer goods drive massive environmental and social impacts. Unilever redesigned portfolio around purpose-led brands aligned with SDGs.
insight: Purpose-driven brands grow faster and generate more profit while addressing social/environmental challenges.
solution: Created 28 Sustainable Living Brands (Dove, Hellmann's, Lipton, Knorr, Omo). 7 of top 10 brands are purpose-driven. Helps 1B+ people improve health, halves environmental footprint.
results: Sustainable Living Brands grew 69% faster than rest of business in 2018, delivering 75% of company growth. Mangrove restoration campaign drove 12% sales uplift.
source: Unilever Sustainability Reports & HBS Case Study

name: Circular Packaging Innovation
context: Plastic packaging drives pollution and waste. Nestlé redesigned operations to minimize virgin plastic while increasing recycled content.
insight: Systemic packaging redesign combining reduction, recycled content, and circular systems can scale sustainability across a massive portfolio.
solution: 83.5% of packaging designed for recycling. 9.3% recycled plastic content (1.6% increase from 2022). 30% recycled plastic in shrink film. 190-tonne annual virgin plastic reduction.
results: 13.58% reduction in net emissions. 91.9% renewable electricity. Collaborated with Ellen MacArthur Foundation on circular principles.

name: Join the Refill Movement
context: Beauty industry drives massive packaging waste. L'Oréal launched global campaign to normalize refillable products across beauty industry.
insight: Cultural marketing can shift consumer behavior toward circular consumption at scale.
solution: Multi-brand, multi-category campaign #JoinTheRefillMovement. Expanded refillable options 17-fold over 5 years. 50% recycled/biobased content target by 2025.
results: Made refilling a beauty ritual. €100M L'AcceleratOR program funding sustainable startups. Halved GHG per product since 2025 target (ahead of schedule).

name: It's Our Home Campaign
context: Consumer behavior drives 70%+ of environmental impact of P&G products. Campaign aimed to shift daily habits toward sustainability.
insight: Simple product innovations combined with consumer education can drive millions to reduce their environmental footprint.
solution: Tide Cold Certified (90% energy reduction). Fairy #SwitchtoShort campaign (1/3 carbon reduction). Tide evo recyclable paper pack. Mr. Clean refill system.
results: 100% recyclable/reusable packaging and 50% virgin plastic reduction targets by 2030. Responsible sourcing of palm, paper, pulp with human rights respect.

name: World Without Waste Initiative
context: Single-use beverage packaging drives massive ocean plastic pollution. Coca-Cola launched system-wide circular economy initiative.
insight: Circular systems require coordinated action across industry, government, and nonprofits. Transparency about evolving goals matters more than perfection.
solution: Originally targeted 50% recycled material by 2030 and 100% collection by 2030. Updated 2024: 30-35% recycled plastic by 2035, 70-75% collection by 2035.
results: Partnered with Ellen MacArthur Foundation, WWF, Ocean Conservancy. Bill Nye animated content demystifying recycling. Discontinued reuse target in 2024.

name: Sustainability Accelerator Program
context: Startups lack capital and networks to scale climate solutions. Amazon launched accelerator to fast-track sustainable innovations.
insight: Corporate resources applied to startup acceleration can unlock solutions faster than traditional venture approaches.
solution: Selected 8 startups from 1,000+ applications. Examples: Morrow (beanless coffee from upcycled ingredients), ReBorn (waste to recyclable homeware).
results: 25+ supported startups. $1M+ in grants/credits. Participants increased sales 700%, raised £16M+. 31,400 electric delivery vans deployed (19,000 in 2023).

name: Sustainable Wash-Off Adhesive for PET Recycling
context: Labels with traditional adhesives contaminate PET bottle recycling streams. Henkel developed wash-off adhesive enabling clean recycling.
insight: Supply chain innovation can remove hidden barriers to circular systems, enabling large-scale material recovery.
solution: Aquence PS 3017 RE water-based acrylic pressure-sensitive adhesive for olefinic filmic labels. Significantly improves PET recycling process.
results: Won FINAT Sustainability Award in 'Optimisation of recycling' category. Launched at Labelexpo 2023. Strong market interest from converters/brand owners.

name: Dettol Refill & Sustainable Product Portfolio
context: Hygiene products drive packaging waste. Reckitt committed to 50% net revenue from sustainable products by 2030.
insight: B2B and B2C sustainability messaging can align profitability with impact when sustainable products offer better value.
solution: Dettol Refill with concentrated formula reducing plastic packaging 75%. Joined Ad Net Zero with Unilever, Google, Meta. 34.9% net revenue from sustainable products (2024).
results: Targeting 50% sustainable product revenue by 2030. 100% renewable electricity and 65% GHG reduction targets by 2030. Joined Ad Net Zero commitment.

name: RE.UNIQLO Clothing Recycling Program
context: Fast fashion drives textile waste. Uniqlo designed modular recycling system with advanced technology for garment-to-garment cycles.
insight: Advanced material science combined with collection infrastructure enables true textile-to-textile recycling at scale.
solution: Developed Recycled Down Jackets from collected items (90% recovery rate). 18-category sorting at facilities. Partnership with UNHCR for aid clothing.
results: Recycled Down represents first product-to-product cycle. Toray separation technology recovers feathers from zippers/fasteners. Supports refugee camps through UNHCRA.

name: HP Instant Ink & Sustainable Printing Solutions
context: Printing industry generates massive waste through inks, plates, and media. HP redesigned printing toward circular systems.
insight: Service models (subscription) can incentivize manufacturers to reduce material use and enable product recovery.
solution: HP Instant Ink reduces carbon 73%, energy 69%, water 70%. HP Indigo press eliminates 80-90% media waste. Take-back programs in 76 countries.
results: 30% postconsumer recycled content plastic target by 2025 (26% achieved in 2024 = 48,840 tonnes). Case: Incapto coffee pouches reduced waste vs capsules.

name: Sustainable Wind Turbine Manufacturing
context: Wind turbines themselves require sustainable manufacturing. Vestas pioneered circular design enabling blade recycling and low-carbon materials.
insight: Renewable energy companies must reduce their own environmental footprint to achieve net-zero credibility.
solution: 85% recyclable turbines. CETEC thermoset composite recycling project. ArcelorMittal partnership: 100% scrap steel powered by wind (52% CO2 reduction).
results: Turbines avoid 455M tons CO2e over lifetime (50x lifetime emissions). 45% supply chain emissions reduction target by 2030. Zero-waste turbines target by 2040.

name: Biomimetic Packaging & Circular Systems
context: Plastic packaging is both critical for food safety and major waste source. Danone developed nature-inspired lighter packaging.
insight: Biomimicry combined with circular systems can reduce material use while improving recycling economics.
solution: Biomimetic packaging for 100g dairy products (8% weight reduction). Partnered with CITEO on reuse system in France. 24% recycled PET in 2023 (50% target by 2025).
results: 693,156 tonnes plastic used in FY2023 (lower than 2022). 15% recovered plastics in non-PET packaging. Settled greenwashing cases, committed to plastic footprint transparency.

name: Transformation from 90% Fossil to 90% Renewables
context: Energy companies dominate fossil fuels. Ørsted transformed business from 90% fossil fuels to 90% renewables in one decade.
insight: Radical business transformation can be profitable. Community benefit initiatives reduce opposition to renewable projects.
solution: Divested oil/gas business. Invested aggressively in offshore wind (world's largest producer by 2019). Closed coal plants by 2023. Community benefit initiatives for social license.
results: Achieved 85% renewable target in 2019 (21 years early). Named world's most sustainable company 2020. Remained highly profitable during transition.
source: Ørsted Sustainability Reports & McKinsey Case Study

name: One for One Social Enterprise Model
context: Billions lack basic shoes for safety, work, and school access. TOMS pioneered business model aligning profit with social impact.
insight: Transparent giving models built into core business create lasting brand loyalty and measurable impact.
solution: Evolved from One for One (one shoe donated per pair sold) to giving 1/3 net profits to organizations building equitable futures.
results: Inspired social enterprises like Warby Parker and Bombas. Impact grants support grassroots organizations. Impact integrated into employee DNA.

name: Buy a Pair, Give a Pair Global Program
context: 500 million people lack access to affordable vision care, limiting work and education potential. Warby Parker distributed glasses globally.
insight: Scalable access models combining direct assistance and social entrepreneurship create sustainable impact.
solution: Distributed 15M+ glasses globally across 80+ countries through VisionSpring partnership and Pupils Project school program.
results: Surpassed 15M pairs distributed by 2023. 40+ US cities served. VisionSpring creates jobs enabling sustainable income for communities.

name: Wind Turbine Blade Recycling with Veolia
context: Wind turbine blades are difficult to recycle, creating waste as first-generation turbines reach end of life. GE partnered on solution.
insight: Partnerships with specialized waste companies can unlock circular opportunities for complex materials.
solution: Agreement with Veolia to recycle onshore wind turbine blades into cement manufacturing feedstock. 27% CO2 reduction in cement production.
results: Transforms end-of-life blades into valuable raw materials. 49,000+ GE turbines installed globally. Digital Wind Farm technology boosts production 20%.

name: Water Access & Net-Zero Technology
award: First Water Solutions Company with SBTi Net-Zero
context: Water scarcity and inefficient pumping drive energy waste and limit access. Grundfos developed energy-efficient solutions.
insight: Pump efficiency improvements can reduce industrial energy use by 37%, enabling both climate action and water access.
solution: E-pumps use 37% less energy than standard pumps. Enabled 17.2M people to access water in 2024. Platinum EcoVadis 2025 (top 1% of 130,000+ companies).
results: 1.5B cubic meters water saved by end users. 50% GHG reduction target by 2030. First water solutions company with SBTi-approved net-zero targets.

name: Modular Long-Life Smartphone Design
context: Smartphones drive e-waste and conflict minerals. Fairphone pioneered modular, repairable phones with transparent supply chains.
insight: User-replaceable components enable 95% CO2 reduction through repair vs replacement, while supporting fair labor.
solution: Modular, customizable phones repairable by users or local businesses. Fairphone 5: replacing cracked screen saves 24kg CO2e. Electronic waste neutral.
results: 2020 LCA shows 95% potential CO2 reduction in some modules through swaps. Conflict-free materials (tin, tungsten). Reuse/Recycle Program collects e-waste.
source: Fairphone Impact Reports & Circular Economy Case Studies

name: Plant-Based Eco-Friendly Cleaning Products
context: Chemical cleaners pollute water and harm health. Seventh Generation scaled plant-based cleaning products from 1988 founding.
insight: Transparency about ingredients combined with plant-based formulas can drive consumer behavior toward safer, sustainable products.
solution: 100% plant-based formulas. 97% packaging reusable/recyclable/compostable by volume. USDA BioPreferred certified 95%+ for laundry/dish products.
results: Recognized as one of 50 most sustainable companies (2018). B Corp & Climate Pledge Friendly certified. All ingredients listed on packaging.
source: Seventh Generation Website & B Lab Directory

name: Science-Based Net-Zero & Sustainable Sourcing
award: First Global Grocer with SBTi Net-Zero
context: Grocery industry drives 90%+ emissions in supply chains. Aldi became first major grocer with science-based net-zero targets.
insight: Systemic supply chain transformation requiring supplier partnerships and on-farm innovation can deliver scale at pace.
solution: Science-based net-zero targets covering value chain. 90% Scope 1&2 emissions reduction by 2035. Dairy/beef supply chain focus. 38% packaging weight reduction.
results: 23% more packaging reduction than 2025 goal. 90% waste diversion from landfills target. New Supplier Engagement Strategy on Climate.

name: Naked & Packaging-Free Beauty Products
context: Beauty packaging drives waste. Lush pioneered 30-50% packaging-free product line with fresh formulations.
insight: Innovative product formats (solid/naked) combined with transparency can enable sustainable scaling.
solution: 30-50% naked products (packaging-free) depending on market. 89% recycled content in all packaging. 100% vegetarian, 80% vegan. Hand-made fresh.
results: Avoided 14,000 tonnes plastic since 2005 + 180M bottles saved. 'Bring It Back' program enables waste-free beauty. 65 tons CO2 saved annually per packaging swap.

name: Circular Carpet Tile with ReEntry Program
context: Flooring generates massive waste. Interface pioneered circular carpet tile business with 15-year warranty and closed-loop recovery.
insight: Extended warranties combined with take-back programs enable profitable circular business models.
solution: ReEntry program reclaims 31,750+ tons post-consumer tile since 2016. 61% recycled/bio-based content. Net-Works collects ocean ghost nets for nylon.
results: 76% carbon footprint reduction since 1996. All products carbon neutral full lifecycle. First global flooring manufacturer with this claim.

name: M0.0NSHOT Zero Net-Zero Carbon Shoe
context: Footwear drives emissions and waste. Allbirds created first net-zero shoe without offsets through material and process innovation.
insight: Transparent carbon accounting combined with sustainable materials enables net-zero products at consumer price points.
solution: M0.0NSHOT Zero sneaker: 1kg CO2 footprint including transport/end-of-life. Uses SweetFoam (recycled sugarcane). Limited edition 500 pairs.
results: World's first net-zero shoe without offsets. Priced at $200. Released in NYC, London, Dubai, Seoul, Tokyo. Inspires industry on carbon reduction.

name: Circular Buy-Back & Rental Services
award: WEF Industry Net Zero Accelerator Case Study
context: Sports gear consumption drives waste. Decathlon transitioned from pure product sales to circular services (buy-back, repair, rental).
insight: Profitability can increase when companies move from product ownership to usage-based models.
solution: Buy-back scheme on 150+ product types. Repair/DIY workshops. Rental for bikes, tents, skiing equipment. AI generative design for material reduction.
results: 2-year warranty all products, 10-year on hiking backpacks. 57 reference frameworks for sustainability. Partner in The Fashion ReModel initiative.
source: Decathlon Sustainability Reports & IMD Case Study 2023-2024

name: SAF Dendê Agroforestry Palm Oil Innovation
context: Palm oil production drives deforestation and emissions. Natura pioneered agroforestry approach regenerating nature while producing oil.
insight: Agroforestry systems can produce commodities while regenerating carbon and biodiversity, creating shared value.
solution: SAF Dendê project: palm oil produced via agroforestry regenerating nature + capturing carbon. Carbon footprint <1/3 of conventional palm.
results: Protects 2.2M hectares Amazon over 20 years. 11,000+ families positively impacted. 40+ Amazon communities engaged. Recognized by UN as Climate Action Champion.

name: Social Justice & Advocacy Campaigns
context: Business can amplify social justice movements. Ben & Jerry's aligned flavor innovations with systemic change campaigns.
insight: Authentic brand activism that connects to core business creates loyal customers and drives systemic change.
solution: Limited-edition flavors (Justice ReMix'd, Change the Whirled). Milk with Dignity program. 7.5% pre-tax profits to community organizations ($1.8M annually).
results: Fairtrade sourcing across supply chain. Regenerative agriculture through Caring Dairy. $1.8M annual community investment since 1985.
source: Ben & Jerry's Website & Business Case Studies

name: Access to Medicine & Health Equity Programs
context: 1B+ people lack access to essential medicines. J&J applied portfolio approach to increase equitable access.
insight: Lab-to-last-mile thinking integrated into R&D from discovery phase enables sustainable access at scale.
solution: Access pricing principles across portfolio. Lab-to-last-mile approach for TB, HIV, Ebola, parasites. 100-org collaboration on historically marginalized communities.
results: Reached 1B+ lives. Ranked #2 in 2022 Access to Medicine Index (6 consecutive top-3 rankings). Americas Health Corps partnership with USAID.
source: J&J Sustainability Reports & Access to Medicine Index

name: 1-1-1 Philanthropic Model & Pledge 1%
context: Most corporate giving is disconnected from core business. Salesforce created model integrating equity, tech, time, and profit.
insight: Integrated giving models combining financial, intellectual, and human capital create exponential impact compared to cash-only giving.
solution: 1-1-1 model: 1% equity, 1% product, 1% employee time to nonprofits. Pledge 1% movement: 10,000+ companies, $1B+ value generated.
results: Power of Us program: discounted tech for nonprofits/education. $1B+ volunteer hours + product + funding since inception. 100+ countries participating.

name: AI-Powered Circular Economy & Supply Chain Solutions
context: Circular economy systems require data integration across complex supply chains. IBM developed AI solutions enabling circular models.
insight: AI can unlock hidden circular opportunities by analyzing data across fragmented supply chains.
solution: Maximo Application Suite: IoT, analytics, AI for asset lifecycle optimization. Envizi: track energy across 600+ locations. Blockchain for recycled material verification.
results: Downer (rail): 51% reliability increase via predictive maintenance. Hera Spa (waste): AI vision reduced landfill across 89 facilities. Asahi Kasei: blockchain recycled plastic tracking.

name: Follow the Liters
context: 785 million people lack access to basic drinking water. Traditional water purification is expensive and complex.
insight: Every consumer purchase can directly fund clean water access in developing countries through a transparent one-for-one model.
solution: For every LifeStraw product sold, a child receives safe drinking water for an entire school year. Follow the Liters program provides transparent tracking of impact.
results: Over 5 million children receive safe water annually. Products used in 64+ countries. Prevented over 168 billion liters of contaminated water from being consumed.

name: Warka Water Tower
context: Rural Ethiopian communities walk hours daily to collect water from unsafe sources.
insight: Atmospheric water harvesting using local materials and ancient knowledge can provide clean water without electricity or infrastructure.
solution: Bamboo structures collecting up to 100 liters of water daily from fog and condensation. Built by local communities using available materials.
results: Multiple towers deployed across Ethiopia and other countries. Provides clean water at near-zero operating cost.

name: Environmental Insights Explorer
context: Cities produce 70% of global CO2 but lack granular data to take targeted action.
insight: Google's mapping data can give every city a carbon emissions blueprint — making invisible pollution visible and actionable.
solution: Free platform providing city-level building and transport emissions data using Google Maps and AI. Covers 40,000+ cities globally.
results: Used by 40,000+ cities worldwide. Enabled data-driven climate action plans. Helped cities identify highest-impact reduction opportunities.

name: Quayside Smart District
context: Cities need radical rethinking to become sustainable — but technology-driven urban planning raises privacy concerns.
insight: A neighborhood built from scratch can prove that sustainability and quality of life are not trade-offs but reinforcing goals.
solution: Planned waterfront neighborhood with mass timber construction, underground freight delivery, adaptive traffic signals, and district energy systems.
results: While the full project was scaled back, it pioneered concepts now adopted globally: mass timber buildings, modular infrastructure, digital urban twins.

name: Plastic Fishing Tournament
context: Mexican fishing communities suffer from ocean plastic but see cleanup as unprofitable work.
insight: If you pay fishermen to catch plastic instead of fish, cleanup becomes a livelihood — not a burden.
solution: Organized fishing tournaments where the catch was plastic, not fish. Fishermen earned money per kilo of plastic collected. Scaled across Mexican coastline.
results: 3 million kg of plastic collected. Fishermen earned supplemental income. Program expanded to Brazil and other Latin American countries.

name: Seabin Project
context: Marina and port areas accumulate floating debris but manual cleanup is expensive and inconsistent.
insight: A simple floating trash can in the water — working 24/7 — can collect what manual cleanup misses.
solution: Automated floating debris collection units installed in marinas and ports worldwide. Each unit filters up to 1.4 tons of debris per year.
results: 900+ Seabins in 53 countries. Over 4,000+ kg of debris collected daily across the network. Data used for pollution mapping.

name: Democracy Is In Your Hands
context: Voter suppression laws disproportionately affect communities of color in the United States.
insight: An ice cream brand can use its cultural reach and retail footprint to drive civic engagement where traditional advocacy cannot.
solution: Launched voter registration drives in stores, created limited-edition flavors linked to voting rights, used packaging and social media for civic education.
results: Registered 25,000+ new voters. Generated massive media coverage on voting rights. Built coalition with 100+ organizations.

name: Too Good To Go
award: B Corp, EU Award for food waste reduction
context: One-third of all food produced globally is wasted, contributing 8-10% of greenhouse gas emissions.
insight: An app connecting consumers with surplus food from restaurants and shops turns waste into affordable meals.
solution: Marketplace app where restaurants, bakeries, and supermarkets sell surplus food in surprise bags at a third of the original price.
results: 350+ million meals saved. 185,000+ partner stores in 19 countries. Over 100 million app users. Prevented equivalent of 900,000 tonnes of CO2.
source: Too Good To Go Impact Report 2024

name: LEGO Replay
context: Millions of LEGO bricks sit unused in homes while children in underserved communities lack educational toys.
insight: LEGO bricks are nearly indestructible — a secondhand brick plays exactly like a new one. Reuse extends joy without new production.
solution: Donation program where families send used LEGO to be cleaned, sorted, and redistributed to children's organizations, schools, and communities in need.
results: Over 400 tonnes of LEGO bricks donated. Reached 500,000+ children. Diverted bricks from landfill while expanding access to creative play.

name: Ecosia Search Engine
context: Internet searches consume energy and generate revenue — but that revenue could fund environmental restoration instead of shareholder returns.
insight: If a search engine donates 100% of profits to tree planting, every search becomes an act of reforestation.
solution: Search engine using ad revenue to fund tree-planting projects worldwide. Transparent financial reporting shows exactly where money goes.
results: 200+ million trees planted. 20+ million active users. Carbon-negative company. Funded projects in 35+ countries.

name: Notpla Seaweed Packaging
context: Single-use plastic packaging pollutes oceans and takes centuries to decompose.
insight: Seaweed grows fast, absorbs CO2, and can be engineered into packaging that biodegrades in weeks — not centuries.
solution: Edible and biodegradable packaging made from seaweed. Used at London Marathon for water capsules, and as takeaway food containers at scale.
results: Won Prince William's Earthshot Prize. Replaced 36,000+ single-use plastic bottles at London Marathon. Partnership with Just Eat for takeaway containers.

name: Slave Free Chocolate
context: Over 1.56 million children work in hazardous conditions in West African cocoa farming.
insight: If you pay cocoa farmers a living income premium — not just Fairtrade minimum — you eliminate the economic root cause of child labor.
solution: Direct trade model with higher-than-Fairtrade prices. Open-source Tony's Open Chain allows other brands to adopt the same supply chain principles.
results: Market leader in Netherlands. $200M+ revenue. 0% child labor in direct supply chain. 30+ partner companies joined Open Chain.
